metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Female
Competitive Agreeableness (28th pct) — Competitive, skeptical, challenging
Low C Conscientiousness (25th pct) — Flexible, spontaneous, disorganised
Introverted Extraversion (29th pct) — Introverted, reserved, solitary
Highly neurotic Neuroticism (94th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
Open Openness to Experience (66th pct) — Open, curious, imaginative, creative
  Low Tender-Mindedness (24th pct) — Tough-minded, unsympathetic, objective
  Low Achievement Striving (31st pct) — Easygoing, content with modest achievement
  Low Self-Discipline (39th pct) — Procrastinates, easily distracted
  Low Activity Level (36th pct) — Leisurely pace, relaxed
  Low Excitement Seeking (21st pct) — Cautious, avoids stimulation
  Very Low Positive Emotions (18th pct) — Less exuberant, serious
  Very High Anger / Hostility (97th pct) — Easily angered, irritable, hostile
  Very High Self-Consciousness (94th pct) — Self-conscious, shy, easily embarrassed
  Highly neurotic Neuroticism (96th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
  Moderate Chronological Age (48th pct)

message.reasoning:
We are to simulate a baseline opinion on "Defense Spending Increase Support". The profile is a 48-year-old female with very high neuroticism (94th pct), low agreeableness (28th pct), low extraversion (29th pct), low conscientiousness (25th pct), and high openness (66th pct). Key facets: very high anxiety, anger/hostility, stress vulnerability; low tender-mindedness, low positive emotions, low excitement seeking, low dutifulness.

This person is likely to be anxious and see threats, but also has low trust (low agreeableness) and low tender-mindedness, making her skeptical of government spending. She is also low on dutifulness and conscientiousness, so not inclined to support increased spending as a matter of principle. High anxiety could lead to support for defense if she perceives threats, but low trust and high hostility might make her suspicious of military expansion. She is also low on positive emotions, so less optimistic about benefits. Overall, this profile suggests a moderate opposition to defense spending increase, because while anxiety might push toward security, her low agreeableness and low trust make her doubt the motives or efficiency. She might lean slightly against. Let's pick a score around -250 to -400, but not extreme. Let's say -312, indicating mild opposition but with reservations. Confidence moderate, around 0.65. Reasoning: low trust and low tender-mindedness dampen support despite high anxiety.
